behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` does not match raw body; do not forward event.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires an external HTTP error contract (`401`) plus proving no enqueue side effect from a real request path. Unit-only tests can miss middleware/body-raw/signature handoff failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service, send a real HTTP webhook with tampered body/signature, assert response is `401`, then read queue state and confirm no new event was persisted.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across external response and persistent side effect; must prove round-trip visibility that persistence happened before ack.
behaviors.test_description: Send a real HTTP webhook; capture ack timing and immediately read persistent queue via real read path; assert event is already present before/at ack completion and status is 200.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream async processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is primarily module orchestration (handler vs async processor lifecycle). No explicit requirement for real network/OS contract beyond internal non-blocking handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + a downstream processor stub that blocks until released; invoke request path and assert response completes before processor completion, while event still gets handed off.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System: behavior text does not require validating a specific external response shape/header or real infra-only failure mode; it focuses on internal wait semantics.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even under slow concurrent downstream work.
behaviors.justification: SLA is wall-clock under concurrency. Real scheduling, queue pressure, and request handling timing can differ from mocked/in-process timing fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Start service with intentionally slow workers processing prior events, generate concurrent webhook traffic over real HTTP, and assert each new webhook response latency stays under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID is not written to persistent queue twice.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module state/dedup interaction (handler + persistence logic). Usually reproducible with real local persistence wiring without requiring external network/system contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Use real handler-to-persistence wiring with local persistent store; submit same event ID twice and assert only one persisted queue record exists.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System: behavior text does not explicitly require external HTTP contract validation or other SYSTEM TRIAD trigger beyond internal persistence correctness.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 and include `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification: Explicit external error-path response contract (status + header) is required, which matches SYSTEM TRIAD error-contract verification.
behaviors.test_description: Force real queue-full condition, send webhook over HTTP, assert response is `503` and `Retry-After` header is present/valid.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow events.
behaviors.justification: Capacity enforcement is mainly queue-module + persistence wiring behavior; local real DB/filesystem integration is the key extra reality signal beyond unit tests.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with small configured capacity on real local persistence backend, enqueue until limit, then attempt one more and assert deterministic rejection without corruption.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System: behavior text does not require external API/HTTP contract checks or subprocess/OS-specific effects beyond local persistence integration.
